# UC-15.1.20 · PDU Branch Circuit Alerts

## Description

Branch breakers trip before mains; per-branch monitoring catches overload before rack outage.

## Value

Branch breakers trip before mains; per-branch monitoring catches overload before rack outage.

## Implementation

Map branches to rack groups. Alert at 80% sustained. Correlate with planned equipment adds.

## Detailed Implementation

Prerequisites
• Install and configure the required add-on or app: Smart PDU branch monitoring.
• Ensure the following data sources are available: `sourcetype="snmp:pdu:branch"` (branch_id, branch_amps, branch_rated_amps).
• For app installation, inputs.conf, and Splunk directory layout, see the Implementation guide: docs/implementation-guide.md

Step 1 — Configure data collection
Map branches to rack groups. Alert at 80% sustained. Correlate with planned equipment adds.

Step 2 — Create the search and alert
Run the following SPL in Search (then save as report or alert; adjust time range and threshold as needed):

```spl
index=power sourcetype="snmp:pdu:branch"
| eval pct=round(branch_amps/branch_rated_amps*100,1)
| where pct > 80 OR branch_status="alarm"
| table pdu_id, branch_id, branch_amps, branch_rated_amps, pct
| sort -pct
```

Understanding this SPL

**PDU Branch Circuit Alerts** — Branch breakers trip before mains; per-branch monitoring catches overload before rack outage.

Documented **Data sources**: `sourcetype="snmp:pdu:branch"` (branch_id, branch_amps, branch_rated_amps). **App/TA** (typical add-on context): Smart PDU branch monitoring. The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed—rename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

The first pipeline stage scopes events using **index**: power; **sourcetype**: snmp:pdu:branch. That sourcetype matches what this use case lists under Data sources.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

• Scopes the data: index=power, sourcetype="snmp:pdu:branch". Cross-check against **Data sources** above so indexes and sourcetypes match your ingestion.
• `eval` defines or adjusts **pct** — often to normalize units, derive a ratio, or prepare for thresholds.
• Filters the current rows with `where pct > 80 OR branch_status="alarm"` — typically the threshold or rule expression for this monitoring goal.
• Pipeline stage (see **PDU Branch Circuit Alerts**): table pdu_id, branch_id, branch_amps, branch_rated_amps, pct
• Orders rows with `sort` — combine with `head`/`tail` for top-N patterns.


Step 3 — Validate
Compare with the PDU UI or SNMP poll for the same devices, readings, and time range.


Step 4 — Operationalize
Step 4 — Operationalize
Step 4 — Operationalize
Step 4 — Operationalize
Add the search to a dashboard or set up alert actions (email, webhook, PagerDuty, etc.) as required. Document the use case in your runbook and assign an owner. Consider visualizations: Bar chart (branch load %), Table (branches at risk).

## SPL

```spl
index=power sourcetype="snmp:pdu:branch"
| eval pct=round(branch_amps/branch_rated_amps*100,1)
| where pct > 80 OR branch_status="alarm"
| table pdu_id, branch_id, branch_amps, branch_rated_amps, pct
| sort -pct
```

## Visualization

Bar chart (branch load %), Table (branches at risk).
